# The Encyclopedia

*A guide to the auto-generated lore encyclopedia. Estimated listening time: 7 minutes.*

---

## What the Encyclopedia is

The Encyclopedia is a World Anvil-style reference that generates itself from your story instance. Every character, place, object, faction, event, and world rule in your lore becomes a browseable article, with cross-references computed automatically.

There is no separate encyclopedia database. Articles are a pure derivation of your story instance data — every time you edit a character or add a faction, the encyclopedia updates to reflect the change.

Navigate to it via the "Encyclopedia" link, or the /encyclopedia route.

---

## Layout

The page has a two-column layout. On the left is a category sidebar listing all article categories: Characters, Places, Objects, Factions, Events, and World Rules. Each category shows how many articles it contains. Click a category to filter the article list.

On the right is the article reader pane. Click any article in the sidebar list to read it.

---

## Article content

Each article is generated from the entity's data fields, formatted as readable text with section headers.

A character article includes their role, status, arc type, description, traits, motivations, relationships, knowledge, and possessions. A place article includes the type, atmosphere, rules, and connections. A faction article includes the type, goals, members, and relationships.

The generation is deterministic — the same entity data always produces the same article text. This means the encyclopedia is a reliable, consistent view of your lore, not a creative interpretation.

---

## Backlinks

The key feature is automatic backlinking. When one article mentions another entity by name, the system detects it and creates a navigable link. At the bottom of each article is a "Referenced By" section showing every other article that mentions this entity.

Click any backlink to jump to that article. This creates a web of cross-references that mirrors the interconnected nature of your story world. A character article might link to the places they've visited, the objects they possess, and the factions they belong to — and each of those articles links back.

---

## Search

The search bar at the top filters articles by text. Type a name, a keyword, or a phrase, and the list narrows to matching articles across all categories. Search checks both the article title and its content.

---

## How it connects

The Encyclopedia reads from your active story instance. If you don't have an instance loaded, the encyclopedia will be empty. If you switch instances in the Story Workspace, the encyclopedia regenerates for the new instance.

Because articles are derived, not stored, the encyclopedia never gets out of sync with your story data. Edit a character's name in the Story Workspace, and the encyclopedia article updates, along with every backlink that referenced the old name.

This makes the Encyclopedia a good way to review your story world as a reader would experience it — not as a collection of database fields, but as a coherent reference.
